fields.title: TechTribe Social Media App
fields.short_description: A Django-based social media platform with user profiles, posts, friend requests, and a friends-only feed.
fields.description:
TechTribe is a full-stack social media web application built with Django. Users can register and create personal profiles, publish posts, send and accept friend requests, and browse a personalised feed showing content only from their friends.

The project demonstrates end-to-end web development skills — from database design and back-end logic to front-end styling — and was deployed to Heroku with a PostgreSQL production database.
fields.tools_used: Python, Django, HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, SQLite, PostgreSQL, Heroku
fields.outcome:
Delivered a fully functional social networking platform with core features including:
- User registration, authentication, and profile management
- Post creation and friends-only feed
- Friend request system (send, accept, decline)
- Responsive UI built with Bootstrap
- Deployed live on Heroku with PostgreSQL

fields.title: Oracle AML — Payment System Integration
fields.short_description: Led the end-to-end integration of Oracle Anti-Money Laundering (AML) with the bank’s payments and account-opening platforms to identify high-risk individuals and suspicious payment behaviors during onboarding.
fields.description:
Led the end-to-end integration of Oracle Anti-Money Laundering (AML) with the bank’s payments and account-opening platforms to identify high-risk individuals and suspicious payment behaviors during onboarding. The integration enabled faster detection of risky applicants, reduced false negatives, and provided a documented audit trail for regulatory reviewers.

 Role & Responsibilities

- Acted as liaison between Compliance, IT, and Oracle vendor teams.
- Elicited and documented AML integration and compliance requirements.
- Mapped customer and transaction data fields between systems.
- Coordinated design, development, testing, and deployment activities.
- Oversaw UAT and production rollout ensuring readiness of compliance teams.
- Implemented KPI tracking to measure fraud detection and false positive rates.

Business Objectives & Success Criteria

- Comply with AML and KYC regulations.
- Identify high-risk customers during onboarding to prevent fraud and sanctions breaches.
- Reduce detection time from 24 hours to near real-time.
- Minimize false positive rates (target <10%).
- Provide traceable audit logs for regulators and auditors.

Outcome & Impact

- Enhanced AML compliance posture and reduced onboarding risk.
- Increased detection accuracy and reduced false positives by 50%.
- Improved operational efficiency for investigators and compliance officers.


Stakeholders & Governance

Key stakeholders and the governance model used to deliver the project:

- Project Sponsor: Head of Compliance / Chief Risk Officer (CRO) — provided strategic direction and sign-off.
- Primary Users: AML investigators, Onboarding officers, Payments operations — responsible for UAT and acceptance.
- IT Teams: Integration, Security, Data Engineering — implemented APIs, transformations, and logging.
- Vendor: Oracle implementation consultants — configured Oracle AML, assisted with tuning and support.
- Legal & Audit: Reviewed regulatory requirements, data retention, and evidence trail.
- Business Owners: Retail Banking and Payments Product Owners — prioritized requirements and pilot scope.

Governance model:

- Weekly steering committee with Sponsor, Compliance, and IT leads.
- Bi-weekly working group for requirements, rule tuning, and integration issues.
- Decision log maintained in Confluence; change requests tracked in JIRA.

Scope (In-scope / Out-of-scope)

In-scope:

- Real-time feeds from onboarding and payments systems to Oracle AML.
- Customer identity resolution and watchlist (PEP/Sanctions) screening at onboarding.
- Case creation workflows and investigator notification for high-risk matches.
- Operational dashboards and compliance reporting for auditors.
- Runbooks, training, and hypercare support during rollout.

Out-of-scope:

- Full historical transaction migration into Oracle AML (only recent/pilot history used).
- Major redesign of onboarding UX beyond required field validations.
- Replacement of legacy batch AML engines (unless specifically contracted).

Data Model & Mapping (Sample)

Below is a concise sample of source-to-target mappings used to feed Oracle AML. This is a representative extract; the full mapping contains more fields and enrichment notes.

Source Field	        Type	Oracle AML Field	                  Notes
customer_id	        string	CUSTOMER_ID                  Unique identifier from onboarding system
full_name	        string	NAME	            Normalized; split into given/family for fuzzy matching
dob	date	        DOB	Used in identity resolution and age checks
address	                string	ADDRESS	                  Geocoded; country risk lookup applied
id_type	                string	ID_DOC_TYPE.                 Passport/Driver license type
id_number	        string	ID_DOC_NUMBER	           Masked in reports; used for verification
payment_amount	decimal	TRANSACTION_AMOUNT	Triggers threshold rules at onboarding
payment_currency  string	CURRENCY	             Converted to USD equivalent for rule thresholds
ip_address	      string	IP_ADDRESS	     Used for geolocation and device mismatch checks
account_open_ts	timestamp  ONBOARD_TS	Event timestamp for audit trail

Enrichment: External watchlists (sanctions, PEP), adverse media feeds, and internal blacklists were queried during transformation. Matching used exact + fuzzy rules and a scoring threshold for auto-hold.

AML Rules & Decisioning Logic

High-level rule categories, example logic, and outcomes used to automate triage and case creation:

Watchlist Match: Exact or fuzzy match against sanctions and PEP lists. Outcome: HOLD and escalate to investigator if match score > 90.

High-Risk Jurisdiction: If customer's country of residence or source of funds is on a high-risk list and evidence is incomplete. Outcome: ALERT → Manual review.

Transaction Threshold at Onboarding: If initial payment/transfer at account opening > defined threshold (e.g., $10,000 USD equivalent). Outcome: HOLD for EDD and verification.

Identity Inconsistency: DOB/name/address conflicts or missing KYC fields combined with IP geolocation mismatch. Outcome: RISK SCORE increment; if score exceeds threshold → HOLD.

Synthetic/Multiple IDs: Same device/IP/email used to create multiple onboarding applications. Outcome: BLOCK or escalate based on frequency and risk score.

Behavioral Anomaly: Rapid sequence of high-value actions within short time-window after onboarding. Outcome: AUTO-ALERT and investigator notification.

Decision outcomes were standardized as: PASS (allow onboarding), ALERT (flag for manual review but allow), HOLD (stop onboarding pending EDD), BLOCK (deny account creation). Acceptance criteria for each rule were defined with Compliance and tested in UAT.

Implementation Approach & Methodology

Approach: Hybrid Agile delivery with defined governance to balance regulatory review cycles and iterative development.

Phase 0 — Discovery & Compliance Workshops (2–4 weeks): Gather requirements, identify legal/regulatory constraints, define KPIs, and agree on rule taxonomy with Compliance.

Phase 1 — Design (3–6 weeks): Create data mappings, interface contracts (API/MQ schemas), architecture diagrams, and detailed rule specifications.

Phase 2 — Development & Integration Sprints (4–10 weeks):  Build transformation layer, integration adapters, and configure Oracle AML. Use CI for deployment artifacts and unit tests.

Phase 3 — Testing & Validation (2–4 weeks): Integration testing, performance testing to meet SLA, rule tuning with Compliance, and UAT with business users.

Phase 4 — Pilot & Monitoring (2–4 weeks):  Limited production pilot with a subset of onboarding traffic; monitor KPIs and false positives, adjust thresholds.

Phase 5 — Production Rollout & Handover:  Full rollout, training, run-books, and 4-week hyper care with dedicated triage support. Handover to Ops and continuous improvement plan.
fields.tools_used: JIRA (backlog and sprints), Confluence (documentation), Postman (API testing), SQL & Python for data validation, Splunk/ELK for logs and monitoring, Git for source control. Regular demos and compliance sign-offs after each milestone ensured alignment.
fields.outcome: Delivered a real-time AML integration that reduced false positives by 50%, improved detection speed from 24 hours to near real-time, and strengthened regulatory compliance through automated audit trails.

fields.title: ATM Channel Enhancement & Self-Service Kiosk Implementation
fields.short_description: Led the ATM Channel Enhancement and Self-Service Kiosk Integration project as a Technical Business Analyst and Project Manager, supporting Access Bank Plc’s digital transformation initiative. Coordinated requirements gathering, vendor collaboration (NCR, Interswitch, Oracle), and system integration
fields.description:
As part of the bank’s digital transformation initiative, I led the project to enhance the functionality of ATMs and deploy self-service kiosks across select branches. The primary goal was to reduce customer queues and in-branch foot traffic by enabling customers to perform more banking transactions through self-service channels. The initiative aimed to modernize customer experience, optimize branch operations, and strengthen the bank’s omnichannel service delivery.

Access Bank initiated the ATM Channel Enhancement and Self-Service Kiosk Integration project to reduce branch foot traffic by extending more banking services to self-service channels.

As the Technical Business Analyst and Project Manager, I led the requirements gathering, system integration with Oracle FLEXCUBE, vendor coordination (NCR, Interswitch, Oracle), and deployment of self-service kiosks across multiple branches.

The project enabled customers to perform account updates, card requests, interbank transfers, bill payments, and cheque deposits through ATMs and kiosks — reducing in-branch transactions by 45% within six months.

Solution Architecture (High-level)

Key Components:

- Middleware Hub: Managed real-time data exchange between ATMs, kiosks, and core banking.
- Security Layer: Implemented encryption, tokenization, and two-factor authentication.
- Monitoring Tools: Used Splunk for log tracking and SLA monitoring.
- User Interface: Designed for intuitive navigation and ADA compliance.

Business Challenge

Branch congestion was a major operational issue for Access Bank. Customers frequently visited branches for routine services like account updates, debit card reissuance, and interbank transfers.

This resulted in:

- Long queues and poor customer satisfaction scores.
- Inefficient use of teller resources.
- Higher operational costs per transaction.

The business sought to offload these transactions to automated channels (ATMs and kiosks) integrated directly with the core banking system.

Objectives

- Integrate Oracle FLEXCUBE core banking with ATM and Kiosk channels.
- Introduce new self-service capabilities: account update, card requests, and bill payments.
- Reduce branch traffic by 40% within six months.
- Enhance data security and transaction traceability.
- Improve customer experience through faster self-service options.
- Developed project plan, risk register, and communication matrix.
- Managed integration testing and pilot rollout in 10 high-traffic branches.
- Tracked KPIs such as transaction success rate, downtime, and footfall reduction.
- Provide executive reporting to the Head of Channels and Operations.

Business Analysis and Requirements Gathering

Key deliverables included:

- Stakeholder workshops with Digital Banking, IT Infrastructure, and Operations.
- Functional Requirements Document (FRD) specifying services, workflow, and business rules.
- System Requirements Specification (SRS) detailing message formats, integration points, and security protocols.
- Use case definitions for:
    - Account update
    - Fund transfer
    - Bill payment
    - ATM deposit
    - Kiosk-based card request

Implemented Services

ATM Enhancements:

- Funds transfer (intra/inter-bank)
- Utility and bill payments
- Mini-statement and checkbook request
- Account balance printout and address update

Self-Service Kiosk Features:

- Instant card printing and activation
- Account statement printing
- Biometric update and KYC verification
- Cheque deposit with image scanning
- Service request ticketing linked to CRM

Solution Architecture

Below is the high-level architecture showing integration of ATM and kiosk systems with backend services:

[Customer]
↓
[NCR ATM] ---- [Interswitch Switch] ---- [Oracle SOA Suite/API Gateway] ---- [Oracle FLEXCUBE]
↓
[Self-Service Kiosk] ---- [Branch Network LAN] ---- [Oracle FLEXCUBE]

- The ATMs route transactions via Interswitch, conforming to ISO 8583 messages.
- The SOA middleware translates these into SOAP/REST calls consumable by FLEXCUBE APIs.
- The Kiosk interacts through direct API calls to the middleware for account updates, BVN validation, and service requests.
Data Mapping and Integration

Source Field	  Middleware Field	         FLEXCUBE Field	           Description	
acctNo	          customerAccount	 ACCOUNT_ID	          Unique customer account number	
transType	  transactionType	         TXN_CODE	                  Transaction classification	
amount	          transactionAmount	TXN_AMT	                  Amount involved	
channel	          channelID	                CHANNEL_SRC	          Identifies ATM or Kiosk	
response	  responseCode	        STATUS	                          Transaction status code	

API Contract Example

 Sample API for Balance Inquiry

```json
POST /api/v1/account/balance
{
  "accountNumber": "0023456789",
  "channel": "ATM",
  "requestId": "TRX12345"
}

```

Response:

```json
{
  "accountName": "John Doe",
  "balance": 55000.75,
  "currency": "NGN",
  "responseCode": "00",
  "responseMessage": "Successful"
}

```
Device Acceptance Test & Pilot Rollout

- Conducted User Acceptance Testing (UAT) across 5 pilot branches.
- Integrated Splunk dashboards for transaction monitoring.
- KPIs:
    - Footfall reduced by 45%
    - 30% of card requests completed via kiosk
    - 25% of bill payments migrated to ATM channels

Key Achievements & Impact

- Reduced branch traffic by 45% and teller workload by 30%.
- Increased digital channel adoption by 50%.
- Improved operational efficiency and turnaround time.
- Enhanced compliance through transaction logging and audit trails.
- Strengthened customer satisfaction and digital transformation strategy.
- Enabled 24/7 access to key banking services, enhancing accessibility.

Governance & Stakeholders

Sponsor: Head of Retail Banking
IT Steering Committee: CIO, Head of Channels, Operations Lead
Vendors: ATM/Kiosk providers, Network providers
Stakeholders: Branch Managers, Digital Banking Unit, Customer Service.
fields.tools_used: JIRA, Confluence, MS Teams, Oracle FLEXCUBE 14.x, Splunk, Oracle Enterprise Manager, In-house .NET-based app, NCR ATM (via Interswitch Switch)
fields.outcome:
The ATM and Self-Service Kiosk Integration Project modernized the Bank’s retail operations and supported its long-term digital transformation roadmap.

By combining strong business analysis, agile project execution, and technical integration with Oracle FLEXCUBE, the initiative demonstrated how technology can drive operational efficiency and customer empowerment in the financial services sector there by reducing footfall at the branches country wide.

fields.title: Expatriate Tracking System (ETS) Automation
fields.short_description: Led the design and implementation of a centralized Expatriate Tracking System (ETS) to digitize and automate workforce compliance and offshore mobilization processes. The platform replaced fragmented Excel-based tracking with a web-based solution .
fields.description:
Problem Overview (Before Automation)

The company relied heavily on Excel sheets, emails, and manual follow-ups to track expatriates' documents and mobilization status. This created serious operational and compliance risks:

Key Issues:

- Visas, work permits, and Safety cards expired without warning.
- HR, HSE, and Operations used different spreadsheets, causing data inconsistency.
- Mobilizations were delayed because medicals or training certificates weren’t validated on time.
- Preparing compliance reports for internal or client audits took 2–3 days.
- Offshore rotation schedules didn’t link to document validity, creating last-minute delays.

This manual process was not scalable for a company with expatriates deployed across multiple offshore locations.

Goal

Digitize and automate the end-to-end tracking of expatriates to eliminate manual errors, reduce compliance risks, and speed up offshore mobilizations.

Scope

In Scope: Worker onboarding, document capture/verification, permit application & renewal workflow, travel & mobilization clearance, training/HSE checklist, accommodation assignment, rotation planning, site access list, alerts & dashboards, audit logs, role‑based access.

Out of Scope : Payroll, expense claims, procurement.

The Solution: Expatriate Tracking System (ETS)

ETS is a centralized web-based platform that automates:

- Expatriate onboarding
- Document and permit tracking
- Automated expiry reminders and escalations
- Offshore/onshore rotation planning
- HSE training & medical clearance validation
- Mobilization approval workflow
- Real-time dashboards and audit-ready reporting

How ETS Works (High-Level)

1. HR creates an expatriate profile and uploads all required documents.
2. The system automatically validates expiration dates and triggers workflows.
3. HSE updates medicals and training compliance.
4. Operations uses the system to plan mobilizations and rotations.
5. ETS checks if all requirements (visa, medical, training) are valid.
6. Mobilization is approved only if the expatriate meets all conditions.
7. The system publishes an automated Gate Pass / Site Access List for clients.
8. Dashboards show expiring documents, pending mobilizations, and compliance rates.

Why This System Is Critical in Oil & Gas

Oil & Gas operations involve:

- strict governmental regulations
- high HSE risks
- client-specific mobilization requirements
- offshore emergency readiness
- mandatory training certifications (BOSIET, HUET, H2S, First Aid)

ETS ensures regulatory compliance, operational efficiency, and safety readiness, which are all essential for offshore work.
fields.tools_used: Jira, Confluence, Figma, Draw.io, Postman, Power BI, Azure DevOps, SharePoint/Teams
fields.outcome:
After implementation:

- 90% reduction in permit-expiry incidents
- 35% improvement in mobilization processing times
- Audit preparation time reduced from 3 days to same-day
- Zero missed medical/training renewals during the pilot phase
- Clear accountability through full audit trails

fields.title: n8n Multi-Agent Workflow — Handyman Ops Desk
fields.short_description: AI-powered n8n Cloud automation that processes handyman job requests end-to-end: intake parsing with Gemini, rules-based pricing, slot scheduling, and dual-path email dispatch.
fields.description:
This project is a beginner-to-intermediate multi-agent automation built in n8n Cloud using the drag-and-drop GUI — no server setup required. The Orchestrator workflow chains four specialised agent workflows via live webhook HTTP calls, processing a handyman service request from start to finish.

Workflow Architecture:

Agent 1 — Intake: Receives a free-text customer description from a Google Sheets form trigger and sends it to Google Gemini 2.5 Flash, which extracts structured data — service type, address, zip code, urgency level, preferred days, and diagnostic notes — returning clean JSON.

Agent 2 — Pricing: Applies JavaScript rules against a service catalog (faucet replace, ceiling fan, TV mount, garbage disposal). Adjusts for zip-code-based travel costs and adds a 20% rush surcharge for high-urgency jobs. Returns an itemised estimate in USD.

Agent 3 — Scheduling: Queries a live time API (timeapi.io) for the current Chicago timezone, then filters a pool of available slots against the customer's preferred days, returning up to three proposed appointment times.

Agent 4 — Comms: Passes the collated job, pricing, and scheduling data to Gemini, which drafts a friendly, under-120-word confirmation message personalised to the customer.

Orchestrator — Handyman Ops Desk: Triggered by new rows in a Google Sheets form response sheet. Calls each agent in sequence, merges results, then routes through a conditional branch — high-urgency jobs or estimates over $300 trigger an operations alert email; all other jobs receive a client-facing confirmation email via Gmail.
fields.tools_used: n8n Cloud, Google Gemini 2.5 Flash, Google Sheets, Gmail, JavaScript, HTTP Webhooks, timeapi.io
fields.outcome:
Fully automated end-to-end job intake pipeline — from Google Forms submission to dual-path email dispatch — with zero manual intervention required.

Key results:
- AI-structured intake eliminates manual data entry and misclassification errors
- Rules-based pricing engine delivers consistent, transparent estimates with dynamic urgency surcharges
- Preference-aware scheduling returns relevant appointment slots instantly
- Conditional routing ensures high-priority jobs are escalated to operations while standard jobs receive an immediate client confirmation
- All five workflows are modular and independently testable, making them reusable across other service verticals

fields.title: Campus SkillSwap — Student Skill Marketplace
fields.short_description: Full-stack Django marketplace where students post, discover, and book peer skills — built with GitHub Copilot using a Vibe Coding workflow in VS Code.
fields.description:
Campus SkillSwap is a full-stack Django web application built as a student peer-to-peer marketplace where users can post, browse, and book skills or services offered by other students.

The project was built using Vibe Coding — an AI-assisted development workflow in VS Code using GitHub Copilot — demonstrating the ability to rapidly scaffold, extend, and customise a production-ready Django application with AI pair programming.

Core Features:

User Authentication — Full registration, login, and logout system using Django's built-in auth framework. Each user gets a personal dashboard showing their posted skills and incoming booking requests.

Skill Listings — Students can create, edit, and delete skill posts with fields for title, description, category, price (or free), contact preference, and availability status. Skills are connected to their owner via a Django ForeignKey relationship.

Category Filtering — Browse page supports filtering by category (Technology, Design, Writing & Tutoring, Music, Language, Math & Science, Other) with visual badge indicators on each card.

Search — Simple keyword search across skill titles and categories, letting students quickly find what they need.

Review & Rating System — Users can leave star ratings and written reviews on skills they have used, building credibility and trust between students.

Booking / Appointments System — Students can submit session requests directly from a skill detail page. The skill owner receives and manages incoming requests through their dashboard. A dedicated Appointments page tracks all scheduled sessions.

Admin Panel — All models are registered in Django admin, allowing full content management.

UI & Styling — Custom dark-themed design with a purple-to-pink gradient header, colour-coded category badges, availability status indicators (Available / Unavailable / Busy), and a responsive card grid layout built on Bootstrap.
fields.tools_used: Django, Python, Bootstrap, SQLite, GitHub Copilot, HTML, CSS, JavaScript
fields.outcome:
Successfully delivered a fully functional multi-user marketplace application with five core feature areas:

- Authentication system with per-user dashboards and skill ownership
- CRUD skill listings with category taxonomy and availability management
- Peer review and star rating system for trust and credibility
- Booking and appointments workflow covering request submission through to scheduling
- Polished custom UI with dark theme, gradient branding, and responsive card grid

The project demonstrates end-to-end Django development — models, views, forms, templates, URL routing, and admin — as well as practical experience with AI-assisted development (GitHub Copilot / Vibe Coding) to accelerate delivery without sacrificing code quality or learning outcomes.

fields.title: Handyman AI Assistant — LangChain + Gemini
fields.short_description: LangChain agent powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash that handles handyman job enquiries, pulls live pricing from Google Sheets, and books appointments directly into Google Calendar.
fields.description:
Handyman AI Project is a LangChain-powered conversational AI assistant built to automate service booking and pricing for a handyman business. The project integrates Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash model with real-world business tools — Google Sheets for dynamic pricing and Google Calendar for availability and appointment booking.

The project was built by cloning a starter repository and fully customising it to connect to personal cloud resources, demonstrating end-to-end AI agent development with external tool integrations.

Technical Stack:

LangChain + LangGraph — agent orchestration framework managing tool calls, memory, and conversation flow across multiple turns.

Google Gemini 2.5 Flash via langchain-google-genai — the core LLM powering natural language understanding, intent detection, and response generation.

Google Sheets Integration — the agent queries a live Google Sheet to look up service pricing dynamically based on the job type requested by the customer.

Google Calendar Integration — the agent checks Google Calendar availability and creates booking entries directly from the conversation, removing manual scheduling.

Chatbot Versions — the project progresses from a simple single-turn assistant (v1) to a full multi-turn interactive chatbot (v2) with persistent chat history and graceful exit handling.

Environment & Tooling — python-dotenv for credential management, LangSmith for tracing and observability, pytest + pytest-asyncio for testing.
fields.tools_used: LangChain, LangGraph, Google Gemini 2.5 Flash, Google Sheets API, Google Calendar API, Python, LangSmith, python-dotenv
fields.outcome:
Successfully deployed a working AI assistant that handles a full customer interaction cycle — from describing a service need, receiving a price estimate pulled live from Google Sheets, to having an appointment booked directly into Google Calendar.

Key outcomes:
- End-to-end LangChain agent with real external tool integrations (not mocked)
- Live Google Sheets pricing lookup replaces static price lists
- Automated Google Calendar booking eliminates manual appointment entry
- Multi-turn conversation with chat history preserves context across the session
- Demonstrates practical LLM deployment: prompt engineering, tool binding, and agent loop design

fields.title: Video Generation with Nano Banana— A Mother's Grief
fields.short_description: AI-generated 21-second short film using Nano Banana — a grieving mother confronts her gang-member son in a fully prompted, emotionally driven narrative scene.
fields.description:
Ingredients to Video is an AI creative project that uses generative AI tools to produce a short emotional film from scratch — starting with a written scene concept and ending with a fully animated, narrative-driven video.

The Scene:

A grieving mother confronts her only son after losing her husband. She discovers he has joined a gang, and in tears asks him why he is trying to destroy her when he is all she has left. Her son breaks down, apologising and explaining that joining the gang was his way of coping with the overwhelming grief of losing his father — he did not know how else to process the pain. The scene ends with the mother embracing her son, promising to get through it together, and committing to starting therapy as a family.

Production Process:

Step 1 — Character & Scene Design: An AI image generator (Nano Banana) was used to create photorealistic visual ingredients — character appearance, costume, emotional expression, interior location, and cinematic lighting — from detailed text prompts.

Step 2 — Prompt Engineering: An LLM was used to refine and expand the scene description, breaking it into shot-by-shot video prompts with specific direction on emotion, body language, camera angle, and dialogue delivery.

Step 3 — Video Generation: Nano Banana's video generation pipeline animated the scene from the image ingredients and prompts, producing a 21-second cinematic clip.

Step 4 — Scene Extension: The 'Extend' feature was used to carry the emotional arc through to the reconciliation and hug, ensuring narrative continuity across the full scene.
fields.tools_used: Nano Banana, AI Image Generation, AI Video Generation, LLM Prompt Engineering
fields.outcome:
Produced a 21-second AI-generated short film with a complete emotional narrative arc — confrontation, breakdown, apology, and reconciliation — entirely from text prompts and AI-generated image ingredients.

Key outcomes:
- Demonstrated end-to-end AI video production: concept → character design → prompt engineering → video generation
- Photorealistic character and environment created without any real footage or actors
- Prompt engineering techniques used to direct emotion, camera movement, and dialogue pacing
- Scene extended beyond the initial generation to complete the narrative with the mother-son hug
- Showcases practical understanding of generative AI tools for storytelling and creative production

fields.title: Virtual Assistant Creation using Chatbase
fields.short_description: AI-powered virtual assistant built on Chatbase, trained on custom knowledge to answer questions about Rosemary's work and background.
fields.description: Designed and deployed a conversational AI virtual assistant using Chatbase — a no-code/low-code AI platform. The assistant, named Rosemary's Virtual Assistant, was trained on custom knowledge sources covering professional background, skills, projects, and certifications. The chatbot is capable of handling natural language queries from visitors, providing instant, accurate responses about experience and expertise. Configured the agent's persona, response style, and knowledge base entirely within the Chatbase platform.
fields.tools_used: Chatbase, AI/LLM, Prompt Engineering, Knowledge Base Configuration
fields.outcome: Deployed a fully functional AI assistant capable of answering visitor questions about professional background, projects, and skills — with no backend code required.
